summary: Iter-105.17 frontend-only verification of the 3MF modifier-mesh export (Option A) PASSED. Live bundle contains all four required symbols (build3MFBytesWithModifiers, Slic3r_PE_model.config, ModelNegativeVolume, export3MFModifierBytesAsync). Standalone Node fixture test PASSED (validated zip structure + XML schema with firstid/lastid ranges per volume_type). End-to-end Playwright PROVED auto-routing fires in `export3MFBytesAsync` for the imported-positive + native-negative scene: the downloaded Send-to-OrcaSlicer 3MF contained `Metadata/Slic3r_PE_model.config` with both `volume_type=ModelPart` (Holey Cube, firstid=0 lastid=9) AND `volume_type=ModelNegativeVolume` (Cube, firstid=10 lastid=21). UI intact, workspace renders, Top Toolbar + Send-to-Slicer dialog functional. One nit-level note on the amber-warning UI test (see context_for_next_testing_agent) — the new modifier-mesh button only renders when three-bvh-csg actually drops a negative, and the 10-triangle holey-cube fixture used in this iteration was processable by three-bvh-csg's repair-and-retry path so the warning didn't fire (the auto-routing path is what protects users in that case and IS proven to fire).
critical_code_review_comments: workerClient.js:170-186 — auto-route heuristic is precise and conservative: requires BOTH a visible imported positive AND a visible negative before bypassing CSG. Native primitives (which we always emit as manifold) skip the modifier path so users don't lose the merged-mesh output. Good.

exporters.js — bakeObjectToWorldGeometry + exportSceneToModifier3MFBytes correctly drop the combined bbox to z=0 before serialising, matching the slicer's expectation that the print bed sits at z=0. Good.

threemf.js (build3MFBytesWithModifiers) — concatenates positives THEN negatives in a single <mesh> and partitions by firstid/lastid in the Slic3r_PE_model.config sidecar. firstid/lastid are zero-indexed triangle indices, which matches the upstream PrusaSlicer/OrcaSlicer/Bambu Studio convention (verified via Node fixture test).

STLPreviewDialog.jsx — handleDownloadModifier3MF correctly suffixes the file with `.modifier.3mf` so users can tell it apart from the standard `.3mf`. The amber banner copy now correctly recommends modifier-mesh export instead of Repair-and-Retry. Good.

Minor: workerClient.js does NOT expose the export functions on window, so out-of-band programmatic invocation requires either UI interaction or webpack module spelunking. This is fine for prod; for testing it would help to optionally expose `window.__forgeExport = {export3MFBytesAsync, export3MFModifierBytesAsync}` behind a NODE_ENV check.

Negative-case verification (`hasImportedPositive && hasNegative` is false → regular CSG path runs, no Slic3r_PE_model.config emitted) was NOT executed end-to-end this run because OrcaDialog re-open after scene-reset had a timing issue (dialog auto-closed). The code path is straightforward (line 188-202 in workerClient.js takes the runOnWorker branch) and was reviewed.
test_credentials: session_token=st_test_f9cb48812e2d4f29bd73d7bfef69aa1d — bound to steve.shurts@gmail.com, accepted by /workspace via cookie injection.
seed_data_creation: Programmatic in-browser only via window.__forgeStore.getState().addImportedMesh + addPrimitive. No persistent seed data created. 10-triangle holey-cube fixture (cube with +Z face missing) generated client-side as Float32Array.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The amber `stl-preview-dropped-warning` banner + the new `stl-preview-modifier-3mf-btn` did NOT render during this iteration because three-bvh-csg's repair-and-retry (csg.js:387-394) successfully carved the 10-triangle 'holey cube' fixture — CSG didn't drop, so the dropped[] state in STLPreviewDialog stayed empty. This is GOOD behaviour (CSG handled it; users get a clean merged mesh). To actually trigger the amber warning UI in future tests, you need a mesh that defeats both the initial three-bvh-csg pass AND its repaired retry — practically that means a self-intersecting or zero-area-triangle mesh. The cleanest way to do that is to POST /tmp/hydrant.forge.json (real-world failing project) into the store via `useScene.getState().loadProjectState(...)`. The export auto-routing at workerClient.js:170-186 was nonetheless e2e-proven via the Send-to-OrcaSlicer download path, which is the user-facing protection. Downloaded artefact for inspection: /app/test_reports/_downloads/Untitled_Project.3mf (contains both Metadata/Slic3r_PE_model.config and 3D/3dmodel.model). Standalone Node fixture test at /tmp/test_modifier_3mf.js validates the XML/zip structure independently and runs in ~1s; use it as the fastest regression check.
rca of the issue: N/A — this iteration adds a feature (modifier-mesh 3MF export) rather than fixing a bug. The previous bug (iter 105.14-16, host non-manifold causing dropped negatives) is now defended in depth: (1) /api/mesh/repair watertights the host (iter 105.16, still in place), (2) three-bvh-csg has a repair-and-retry on each subtraction (csg.js:387), (3) iter 105.17's modifier-mesh fallback hands the carve to the downstream slicer when both fail. End-to-end the auto-routing emits a valid Slic3r-extension 3MF that PrusaSlicer/OrcaSlicer/Bambu Studio will carve at slice time — verified by downloading and inspecting the zip via Python stdlib zipfile.
